evidence_span: Mosquitoes of the genus Culex are generally considered the principal vectors of WNV.

evidence_span: Culex pipiens and Culex modestus are the main vectors of WNV.

evidence_span: Urban yellow fever transmission occurs between humans and urban mosquitoes primarily Aedes aegypti.

evidence_span: Sylvatic yellow fever involves non-human primates and forest canopy mosquitoes including Aedes africanus.

evidence_span: Yellow fever is transmitted by infected Aedes Haemagogus and Sabethes mosquitoes.

evidence_span: Dengue virus is transmitted to humans primarily by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes.

evidence_span: Yersinia pestis is maintained naturally in a cycle involving wild rodents and fleas.

evidence_span: Yersinia pestis can be transmitted by the Oriental rat flea Xenopsylla cheopis and other flea species.

evidence_span: RVF virus persists through transmission between vectors and hosts and maintains through vertical transmission in Aedes mosquitoes.

evidence_span: RVF transmission can amplify in naive ruminants via local competent mosquitoes like Culex Mansonia and Anopheles that act as mechanical vectors.
